definition: With no qualification; totally confident.
Used to emphasize the truth or facts of a situation.
Used to introduce a concession or statement.
State as a fact strongly and publicly.
Completely; totally.
As far as one knows or can see.
Find something out for certain; make sure of.
State a fact or belief confidently and forcefully.

Confidently; with certainty.

In the most fundamental respects; fundamentally.
For a short time; in a few words.

In a way that is unambiguously explicit and direct.
Undoubtedly; definitely.

In a self-assured way that expresses faith in oneself.

As a result.

Without doubt.

Consider one thing to be the same as or equivalent to another.
Absolutely necessary; extremely important.
Used to emphasize the basic, fundamental, or intrinsic nature of a person, thing, or situation.
Show to be true or certain by determining the facts.
Plainly or obviously; in a way that is clearly seen or understood.
Without discrepancy; used to emphasize the accuracy or truth of a statement.

In a way that is concerned with what is actually the case rather than interpretations of or reactions to it.
With little possibility of movement; securely.
In an open, honest, and direct manner.
Forming a necessary base or core; of central importance.

In a truthful, fair, or honorable way.

At first.
